quizQuestion:
Cuando llegué, ya {___} comido.
(When I arrived, they had already eaten.)
sentenceTranslation: When I arrived, they had already eaten.
explanation: habían — imperfect of haber (ellos), used to form pluperfect (had done). Pluperfect = había/habías/había/habíamos/habíais/habían + past participle. Expresses an action that happened before another past action. Ya habían comido = they had already eaten.

quizQuestion:
Ya {___} llegado cuando llamaste.
(I had already arrived when you called.)
sentenceTranslation: I had already arrived when you called.
explanation: había — imperfect of haber (yo/él/ella). Ya había llegado = I had already arrived. The pluperfect is used for the earlier of two past actions. Ya (already) + pluperfect is a very common B1 pattern.

quizQuestion:
No {___} dormido bien.
(You hadn't slept well.)
explanation: habías — imperfect of haber (tú). No habías dormido bien = you hadn't slept well (past state before another point in time). habías is used only for tú in the pluperfect.

quizQuestion:
{___} terminado antes de las ocho.
(We had finished before eight.)
explanation: habíamos — imperfect of haber (nosotros). Habíamos terminado = we had finished. The pluperfect is used to show one past action happened before another. Habíamos terminado → antes de las ocho (time reference).

quizQuestion:
¿{___} visto esa película antes?
(Had you seen that film before?)
sentenceTranslation: Had you seen that film before?
explanation: habías — imperfect of haber (tú) for pluperfect. ¿Habías visto esa película? = Had you seen that film? A classic B1 pluperfect question using ver (irregular PP: visto). Note: ¿Has visto? (present perfect) vs. ¿Habías visto? (pluperfect).

quizQuestion:
He {___} la ventana.
(I have opened the window.)
explanation: abierto — irregular past participle of abrir. abrir → abierto (not *abrido). Common usage: He abierto (present perfect), La puerta estaba abierta (adjective), Tengo la ventana abierta (result state).

quizQuestion:
La puerta estaba {___}.
(The door was open.)
grammarPointLabel: irregular PP as adjective: abrir → abierto/a
explanation: abierta — past participle of abrir used as adjective. As an adjective, it agrees in gender/number: abierto (masc), abierta (fem), abiertos, abiertas. La puerta (feminine) → abierta.

quizQuestion:
¿Has {___} la carta?
(Have you written the letter?)
explanation: escrito — irregular past participle of escribir. escribir → escrito (not *escribido). Also: describir → descrito (same pattern). Useful sentence: El libro está escrito en español.

quizQuestion:
¿Has {___} los deberes?
(Have you done your homework?)
explanation: hecho — irregular past participle of hacer. hacer → hecho (not *hacido). One of the most common irregular participles: ¿Qué has hecho? (What have you done?), está hecho (it's done), de hecho (in fact).

quizQuestion:
El pájaro había {___}.
(The bird had died.)
explanation: muerto — irregular past participle of morir. morir → muerto (not *morido). Used in present perfect and pluperfect: Ha muerto (has died), había muerto (had died). As adjective: estar muerto/a (to be dead).

quizQuestion:
He {___} el vaso sin querer.
(I have broken the glass by accident.)
sentenceTranslation: I have broken the glass by accident.
explanation: roto — irregular past participle of romper. romper → roto (not *rompido). Adjective usage: La ventana está rota. Present perfect: He roto el plato. Common B1 vocab item paired with accidental actions.

quizQuestion:
La ventana estaba {___}.
(The window was broken.)
grammarPointLabel: irregular PP as adjective: romper → roto/a
explanation: rota — past participle of romper used as feminine adjective. La ventana (fem) → rota. Note agreement: el cristal roto (masc), la ventana rota (fem).

quizQuestion:
¿Has {___} el problema?
(Have you solved the problem?)
explanation: resuelto — irregular past participle of resolver. resolver → resuelto (not *resolvido). Same pattern: volver → vuelto, absolver → absuelto. Common in academic/professional contexts.

quizQuestion:
La mesa está {___} de polvo.
(The table is covered in dust.)
sentenceTranslation: The table is covered in dust.
explanation: cubierto — irregular past participle of cubrir. cubrir → cubierto (not *cubrido). Same pattern: descubrir → descubierto, encubrir → encubierto. La mesa está cubierta (adjective, agrees with fem noun).

quizQuestion:
He {___} las llaves en la mesa.
(I have put the keys on the table.)
sentenceTranslation: I have put the keys on the table.
explanation: puesto — irregular past participle of poner. poner → puesto (not *ponido). Also: componer → compuesto, imponer → impuesto, proponer → propuesto. He puesto = I have put/placed.

quizQuestion:
¿Has {___} del trabajo?
(Have you returned from work?)
explanation: vuelto — irregular past participle of volver. volver → vuelto (not *volvido). Same pattern: resolver → resuelto, devolver → devuelto. Ha vuelto = he/she has returned.
